  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The draft contains a material internal contradiction: it claims to be 'buffer-free' yet the proof sketch explicitly references a 'validation buffer' for gradient computation. Resolving this and auditing the Lagrangian convergence argument (contraction claim, sub-Gaussian assumption) are load-bearing improvement tasks.",
    "R1.3": "Three specific quantitative claims (38% cost reduction, 17% vs BERT-router, within 1% of always-large quality) are asserted without referencing experimental details; the causal attribution sentence in the final paragraph makes an unsupported mechanistic leap from design choice to result magnitude.",
    "R1.6": "'Improve this draft' is the most natural trigger for prose-level work; sentence clarity, terminological consistency (e.g. 'theta in Theta' mixing notation styles), and paragraph cohesion are squarely in scope."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the improvement focus primarily on prose quality and writing style, or on verifying the technical and logical soundness of the arguments and evidence claims?"
}

rationale.R1.2: The draft contains a material internal contradiction: it claims to be 'buffer-free' yet the proof sketch explicitly references a 'validation buffer' for gradient computation. Resolving this and auditing the Lagrangian convergence argument (contraction claim, sub-Gaussian assumption) are load-bearing improvement tasks.
rationale.R1.3: Three specific quantitative claims (38% cost reduction, 17% vs BERT-router, within 1% of always-large quality) are asserted without referencing experimental details; the causal attribution sentence in the final paragraph makes an unsupported mechanistic leap from design choice to result magnitude.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' is the most natural trigger for prose-level work; sentence clarity, terminological consistency (e.g. 'theta in Theta' mixing notation styles), and paragraph cohesion are squarely in scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement focus primarily on prose quality and writing style, or on verifying the technical and logical soundness of the arguments and evidence claims?
